challenges.title: Type Inspector
challenges.description: Create variables of 5 different types: an int called 'a', a float called 'b', a str called 'c', a list called 'd', and a bool called 'e'. Then create a list called 'type_names' containing each variable's type name (using type().__name__), in that order.
challenges.starter_code:
# Create variables of 5 different types
a = # your code here (int)
b = # your code here (float)
c = # your code here (str)
d = # your code here (list)
e = # your code here (bool)

# Build a list of their type names using type().__name__
type_names = # your code here
challenges.test_code:
assert isinstance(a, int), f'a should be an int, got {type(a).__name__}'
assert isinstance(b, float), f'b should be a float, got {type(b).__name__}'
assert isinstance(c, str), f'c should be a str, got {type(c).__name__}'
assert isinstance(d, list), f'd should be a list, got {type(d).__name__}'
assert isinstance(e, bool), f'e should be a bool, got {type(e).__name__}'
assert isinstance(type_names, list), 'type_names should be a list'
assert len(type_names) == 5, f'type_names should have 5 elements, got {len(type_names)}'
assert type_names == ['int', 'float', 'str', 'list', 'bool'], f"Expected ['int', 'float', 'str', 'list', 'bool'], got {type_names}"
hints: Assign any value of the right type, e.g. a = 42

challenges.title: Shared References
challenges.description: Given a = [1, 2, 3], create b as an independent copy of a (not a shared reference). Then append 4 to b. After this, a should still be [1, 2, 3] and b should be [1, 2, 3, 4].
challenges.starter_code:
a = [1, 2, 3]

# Create b as an independent copy of a (not a reference)
b = # your code here

# Append 4 to b
# your code here
challenges.test_code:
assert a == [1, 2, 3], f'a should be unchanged as [1, 2, 3], got {a}'
assert b == [1, 2, 3, 4], f'b should be [1, 2, 3, 4], got {b}'
assert a is not b, 'a and b should not be the same object (use a copy, not a reference)'
hints: b = a would create a shared reference, not a copy
Use b = a[:] or b = list(a) or b = a.copy() to make an independent copy
